evidence_quotes.quote: extra line added for software like videogames for example.
in some programs you can also buy extra items on the software
the software I buy is completely dependent on the servers the company provides.
destroy their owned copies that they bought for.

paragraphs.text: I want to request that there could be an extra line added for software like videogames for example. Companies tend to sell software on something on a disk or digitally which if you purchase it in a shop you would think that you own it. But these companies can apparently enforce their own services on the disk meaning the software I buy is completely dependent on the servers the company provides. When the company ends the service i have a disk that is completely useless and I have to throw it out which would make more waste. Also in some programs you can also buy extra items on the software but if I lose acces because they shutdown their servers I would also lose all the items I have bought with the software included. The companies would then have incentive to make a new, copied software which I have to pay for again with the items which could probably be sold for again without any form of innovation. That is why i propose a law that must require companies to allow consumers to use their products without them deciding when they should destroy their owned copies that they bought for. This would reduce waste and incentivize companies to innovate and make better products. I hope that my feedback will be made into consideration.
